articles.title: India was 5th biggest military spender in 2025, expenditure rose by 8.9% to $92 bn; China’s spend $336bn; Pak $11.9bn: Report
articles.description: India ranked as the fifth largest military spender globally in 2025, with an 8.9% increase to $92.1 billion, influenced by the India-Pakistan conflict. Global military expenditure reached $2,887 billion, an 11th consecutive year of growth, driven by Europe and Asia, despite a US spending decrease.

articles.title: This man was laid off from Amazon at 43. Here's how he got a new job in 90 days
articles.description: The professional described how he relied heavily on networking, not just within his immediate circle but beyond it. 'I ended every single catch-up call with one simple ask: ‘Can you forward my CV to two people in your network who should see it?'' he said, explaining that opportunities often exist within extended professional connections.

articles.title: Textiles, jewellery, leather seen leading gains from India–New Zealand FTA; broader export impact may be limited
articles.description: The agreement, signed on April 27, reduces or eliminates tariffs on 100 percent of Indian exports to New Zealand, while India will offer market access on about 70 percent of tariff lines covering roughly 95 percent of New Zealand’s exports, including wool, coal, wood and wine.

articles.title: Tax saving investments in India: 7 things you need to know
articles.description: Most Indians overpay income tax simply because they do not plan ahead. The Income Tax Act offers multiple legal ways to reduce your tax bill — through Section 80C, 80D, 80CCD, and more. A salaried person in the highest tax bracket can save up to Rs 46,800 in taxes on a single Rs 1.5 lakh investment. The only catch? You need to act before the financial year ends.
